body.text: The Universal Bridge establishes an open-source framework for AI accessibility, ensuring that artificial intelligence tools remain usable by neurodivergent individuals and those with disabilities.

As AI becomes increasingly pervasive, accessibility considerations must be built into the foundation rather than retrofitted as afterthoughts. This paper establishes principles and implementations for truly inclusive AI systems.
Perceivable: Information must be presentable in multiple ways
Operable: Interface components must be usable
Understandable: Information and operation must be comprehensible
Robust: Content must be interpretable by assistive technologies
The Universal Bridge framework applies to all P31 Labs artifacts, ensuring that BONDING, The Buffer, Node Zero, and all future developments remain accessible to neurodivergent users.
